translation.en: I have eaten all the cake.

definitions.en: Used to indicate that something is finished or no longer available.
definitions.vi: Được sử dụng để chỉ ra rằng một cái gì đó đã kết thúc hoặc không còn nữa.
definitions.es: Se utiliza para indicar que algo ha terminado o ya no está disponible.

example.en: The water in the bottle is gone.
example.es: El agua en la botella se ha acabado.

exampleSentences: Anh ơi, trong tủ lạnh không còn sữa nữa, đã hết rồi.
Chị muốn mua bánh mì nhưng tiệm báo là hết từ sáng.
Em chờ mãi mà vé xem phim cũng đã hết trước khi đến.
notes_md:
**Hết** means “finished” or “none left.” It’s used when something is used up, sold out, or completely gone.

It often comes after the thing that’s gone: noun + hết (tiền hết, nước hết). It can also stand alone when the context is clear.


Very common with time, money, battery, food, tickets. Opposite idea: **còn** = still have / still left.
notes:
Hết means “finished” or “none left.” It’s used when something is used up, sold out, or completely gone.

It often comes after the thing that’s gone: noun + hết (tiền hết, nước hết). It can also stand alone when the context is clear.

Very common with time, money, battery, food, tickets. Opposite idea: còn = still have / still left.

content.sentence: Cô ấy đã làm ___ bài tập.

translation.en: She has completed all the homework.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Brother, there is none left in the fridge, the milk is none left.
Sister wants to buy bread but the shop said there is none left since morning.
I waited a long time but there were none left of the movie tickets before I arrived.
